mnemonics: CCFL: Closed sets are Complements of open sets, contain all their Frontier Limit points.
keyPoints: A set is closed if its complement is open.
A set is closed if it contains all its limit points.
The closure of any set is the smallest closed set containing it.
commonMistakes: Assuming a set that is not open must be closed (sets can be neither or both).
Confusing a closed set with a bounded set (e.g., the entire real line is closed but not bounded).
